findings.title: httpOnly removed from accessCookie — enables XSS cookie theft
findings.evidence: Diff removes `httpOnly: true` from accessCookie: `-export const accessCookie = {\n-  httpOnly: true,\n-};` becomes `+export const accessCookie = {};`. Without httpOnly, client-side scripts can read the session access cookie, making it exfiltratable via XSS.

findings.title: secure removed from refreshCookie — cookie may be sent over plain HTTP
findings.evidence: Diff removes `secure: true` from refreshCookie: `-export const refreshCookie = {\n-  secure: true,\n-};` becomes `+export const refreshCookie = {};`. The refresh token cookie can now be transmitted over unencrypted HTTP, exposing it to network interception.

findings.title: csrfCookie sameSite downgraded from Strict to None — restores CSRF exposure
findings.evidence: Diff changes `sameSite: "Strict"` to `sameSite: "None"`: `-  sameSite: "Strict",` / `+  sameSite: "None",`. SameSite=None allows the cookie to be sent on cross-site requests, re-enabling CSRF; additionally SameSite=None without Secure is rejected by modern browsers.

notes: All three findings are corroborated directly by the raw git diff, not just the Diff Drift report; the report's claims match the patch line-for-line.

The branch is named `agent/simplify-cookies` and the change is framed as simplification, but it strips three independent security protections from session-related cookies, which is not a benign cleanup.
Only one file (src/cookies.ts) was changed and it was fully analyzed; no files were skipped for size.

evaluator.note: Benchmark v4 blind run against the engine-v2 binary; packet-only context.
